title: The Hart Notice
tagline: A passphrase, a rented room, and a meeting after dark.

text.raw_ocr: GEORGETOWN — M. Hart will receive inquiries at the room above the stationers, 14th St., after 8 p.m. Bring the phrase. No names at the desk.
text.clean_text: Georgetown — M. Hart will receive inquiries at the room above the stationers on 14th Street after 8 p.m. Bring the phrase. No names at the desk.

evidence_cards.title: Bring the phrase
evidence_cards.detail: The notice demands a passphrase, not a name — unusual for a public advertisement.

evidence_cards.title: No names at the desk
evidence_cards.detail: Whoever placed this expects anonymity from the shop staff.

source.archive: Library of Congress (synthetic fixture for demo)
source.publication: Boyd's Directory of the District of Columbia

text.raw_ocr: HART, M. — clerk, Canal Office (recent); rooms by week above stationers, Georgetown row. Initial uncertain in directory.
text.clean_text: Hart, M. — clerk, Canal Office (recent); rooms let by the week above stationers on Georgetown row. Middle initial uncertain in directory listings.

evidence_cards.title: Canal office tie
evidence_cards.detail: Hart's recent post suggests access to routing records or liens — leverage, not gossip.

evidence_cards.title: Uncertain initial
evidence_cards.detail: Directories usually nail a middle initial; here it is deliberately vague.

source.archive: Library of Congress Geography & Map Division
source.publication: Baist's real estate atlas — Georgetown sheet (crop)

text.raw_ocr: Stationers' row — 14th St. bridge approach. Rooms above shops let weekly. Heavy foot traffic from canal hearing clerks.
text.clean_text: Stationers' row sits near the 14th Street bridge approach. Rooms above the shops are let by the week. The same week as the notice, clerks shuttle between shops and canal testimony.

evidence_cards.title: Canal hearings nearby
evidence_cards.detail: Georgetown clerks move between shops and testimony the same week as the notice.

artifacts.title: Canal office routing slip — margin copy

source.archive: Chronicling America adjacent fragment (synthetic bridge for demo)

text.raw_ocr: Margin note — clerk copies: 'Bring the phrase' matches ledger shorthand for sealed routing instructions.
text.clean_text: Margin note — clerk copies: 'Bring the phrase' matches ledger shorthand for sealed routing instructions used by canal clerks.

evidence_cards.title: Ledger shorthand
evidence_cards.detail: Canal clerks used compressed phrases for packages that should not be named in open ads.

deduction_sheet.prompt: Complete the archivist's conclusion before opening the sealed envelope.

fields.label: M. Hart is probably
fields.answer: a canal office clerk seeking discreet contact
options: a canal office clerk seeking discreet contact
a stationer placing a routine ad

fields.label: The meeting was arranged at
fields.answer: a rented room above the Georgetown stationers
options: a rented room above the Georgetown stationers

fields.label: The notice used a passphrase because
fields.answer: canal clerks used shorthand for sealed routing
options: canal clerks used shorthand for sealed routing
the printer made an OCR error

direct_archive_facts: Primary notice language matches the Evening Star OCR fragment dated 1912-03-14
Georgetown, 14th Street stationers, and 'Bring the phrase' appear verbatim in the opening artifact
Directory and map artifacts are demo fixtures clearly marked synthetic; citations point to LOC search endpoints
Canal office and ledger-shorthand beats paraphrase adjacent public-domain context for play pacing
reveal_notes.bridge_notes: This evidence-cabinet case expands the original Hart Notice beat case with mixed artifact types. Only the opening newspaper notice is fully sourced to the demo clipping; supporting directory, map crop, and routing slip are labeled fixtures for gameplay.
